property.text: Block transactions may contain any supported transaction type (Legacy, AccessList, FeeMarket, Blob, SetCode). The transactions field MUST accept Tuple[Bytes | LegacyTransaction, ...].

reachability.notes: All transaction types must be correctly handled. Missing support for a type causes block rejection.

property.severity_justification: Failure to support a valid transaction type causes nodes to reject valid blocks.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a valid transaction of each type and demonstrate block construction rejects any valid type.

property.text: The Log data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). A Log consists of (address: Address, topics: Tuple[Hash32, ...], data: Bytes).

reachability.notes: Logs are produced during transaction execution. Mutable logs could be tampered with before receipt construction.

property.severity_justification: Mutable logs could be altered between emission and receipt construction, corrupting bloom filters and receipts.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Log object can be mutated after freeze.

property.text: Log topics tuple MUST have at most 4 elements, corresponding to LOG0 through LOG4 opcodes. forall (log : Log): len(log.topics) <= 4.

reachability.notes: The EVM enforces the LOG0-LOG4 opcode limit. A bug in the EVM allowing > 4 topics would corrupt bloom filters.

property.severity_justification: Exceeding the topic limit would cause bloom filter inconsistencies and receipt validation failures.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a log with > 4 topics being emitted and processed without rejection.

property.text: The Receipt data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). A Receipt consists of (succeeded: bool, cumulative_gas_used: Uint, bloom: Bloom, logs: Tuple[Log, ...]).

reachability.notes: Receipts are constructed during block processing and transmitted via P2P.

property.severity_justification: Mutable receipts could be tampered with after construction, corrupting the receipts trie and block validation.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Receipt object can be mutated after freeze.

property.text: The cumulative_gas_used field in Receipt MUST be monotonically non-decreasing within a block. forall (block, i < j): block.receipts[i].cumulative_gas_used <= block.receipts[j].cumulative_gas_used.

reachability.notes: A malicious block builder could order transactions such that cumulative gas decreases, indicating a gas accounting bug.

property.severity_justification: Non-monotonic cumulative gas indicates a gas accounting vulnerability that could allow gas undercharging.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block where cumulative_gas_used decreases between consecutive receipts.

property.text: The bloom filter in a Receipt MUST be consistent with the logs in that receipt. forall (receipt): receipt.bloom == logs_bloom(receipt.logs).

reachability.notes: An inconsistent bloom filter causes false negatives in log queries, affecting dApps and indexers.

property.severity_justification: Bloom filter inconsistency causes silent failure in log filtering, breaking all dApp event subscriptions.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a receipt where bloom does not match logs_bloom(receipt.logs).

property.text: The bloom bytearray MUST be exactly 256 bytes (2048 bits). forall (bloom): len(bloom) == 256.

reachability.notes: An under- or over-sized bloom array would cause index-out-of-bounds errors or incorrect bit-setting.

property.severity_justification: Wrong bloom filter size causes out-of-bounds access (crash) or incorrect bloom (consensus split).
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: trigger a bloom filter operation with a non-256-byte array and demonstrate crash or incorrect behavior.

property.text: In add_to_bloom, the bit_to_set value MUST always be in range [0, 2047] due to the 11-bit mask 0x07FF. forall (hashed, idx): Uint.from_be_bytes(hashed[idx:idx+2]) AND 0x07FF is in [0, 2047].

reachability.notes: If the masking is incorrect, bit indices could exceed the 2048-bit bloom filter, causing OOB writes.

property.severity_justification: Out-of-range bit indices cause memory corruption or consensus-divergent bloom filters.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a bloom entry that produces a bit_to_set outside [0, 2047].

property.text: In add_to_bloom, the byte_index MUST always be in range [0, 255]. forall computation: byte_index = bit_index // 8 where bit_index is in [0, 2047], thus byte_index is in [0, 255].

reachability.notes: Derived from bit_to_set invariant. If bit_to_set is correct, byte_index is automatically bounded.

property.severity_justification: Out-of-range byte index causes array out-of-bounds write, crashing the node.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a byte_index > 255 being computed, causing an out-of-bounds access.

property.text: The add_to_bloom loop MUST iterate exactly 3 times (at indices 0, 2, 4), setting 3 bits per bloom entry. No more, no fewer iterations are permitted.

reachability.notes: Fewer or more iterations produce a bloom filter incompatible with the spec, causing consensus splits.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect number of bit-settings per bloom entry produces a bloom filter that diverges across clients, causing consensus split.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that add_to_bloom sets a number of bits != 3 for any input.

property.text: logs_bloom MUST initialize the bloom bytearray to all zeros (256 bytes) before processing any logs. forall: at the start of logs_bloom, bloom == bytearray(256 * [0]).

reachability.notes: If the bloom is not zeroed, residual bits from previous computations could cause false positives.

property.severity_justification: Non-zeroed bloom initialization causes incorrect bloom filters, leading to consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that logs_bloom reuses a non-zeroed buffer, producing an incorrect bloom.

property.text: logs_bloom MUST add every log address to the bloom filter exactly once per log. forall (log in logs): add_to_bloom(bloom, log.address) is called exactly once.

reachability.notes: Missing or duplicate address additions produce an incorrect bloom filter.

property.severity_justification: Missing an address from the bloom causes false negatives in log filtering.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a log address that is not added to the bloom filter.

property.text: logs_bloom MUST add every topic of every log to the bloom filter exactly once. forall (log in logs, topic in log.topics): add_to_bloom(bloom, topic) is called exactly once.

reachability.notes: Missing topic additions produce incorrect bloom filters, causing false negatives.

property.severity_justification: Missing a topic from the bloom causes false negatives in topic-based log filtering.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a log topic that is not added to the bloom filter.

property.text: logs_bloom MUST return the final Bloom object only after ALL logs have been processed. The bloom MUST NOT be returned prematurely (before processing all logs).

reachability.notes: Early return would produce an incomplete bloom filter missing entries from unprocessed logs.

property.severity_justification: Incomplete bloom filter causes silent false negatives in log queries.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that logs_bloom returns before processing all logs in the input.

property.text: add_to_bloom MUST be called exactly (1 + len(log.topics)) times per log entry: once for the address and once for each topic. forall (log): calls_to_add_to_bloom(log) == 1 + len(log.topics).

reachability.notes: Incorrect call count produces a bloom that is either incomplete or has extra entries.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect bloom call count produces a non-standard bloom filter, causing consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate add_to_bloom is called an incorrect number of times for a given log.

property.text: decode_receipt MUST reject receipts received from P2P peers with type-prefix bytes outside {1, 2, 3, 4} via assertion failure. Specifically: assert receipt[0] in (1, 2, 3, 4).

reachability.notes: A malicious peer can send crafted receipt bytes via devp2p with arbitrary first byte.

property.severity_justification: Bypassing the assertion allows the decoder to process arbitrary byte sequences as receipts, potentially causing memory corruption or state confusion.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a receipt payload with an invalid type prefix that is accepted by decode_receipt.

property.text: A peer MUST NOT be able to send a receipt encoded with a type prefix different from the actual transaction type. Receipt type prefixes MUST be verified against the corresponding transaction type during block validation.

reachability.notes: Type prefix mismatch could cause the execution engine to misattribute receipts to wrong transaction types.

property.severity_justification: Misattributing receipt types corrupts the receipts_root and causes consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: send a receipt with type prefix 0x02 for a BlobTransaction and show it is accepted.

property.text: decode_receipt MUST safely handle extremely large RLP-encoded receipts or deeply nested structures from P2P peers without crashing or consuming excessive memory.

reachability.notes: A malicious peer can send oversized or deeply nested RLP payloads to exhaust memory.

property.severity_justification: Memory exhaustion or CPU exhaustion via crafted RLP payloads causes node crash (DoS).
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: send a crafted RLP payload that causes decode_receipt to consume excessive memory or CPU.

property.text: A bug in the RLP codec MUST NOT produce non-canonical encodings for Receipt objects. All client implementations MUST produce bit-identical RLP output for the same Receipt input. forall (receipt, client_A, client_B): rlp.encode(receipt, client_A) == rlp.encode(receipt, client_B).

reachability.notes: Cross-client RLP encoding differences in receipts cause receipts_root divergence and consensus splits.

property.severity_justification: Non-canonical RLP encoding directly causes consensus splits between clients using different RLP implementations.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a Receipt that encodes to different bytes in two different client implementations.

property.text: The keccak256 implementation used in bloom filter construction (add_to_bloom) MUST produce correct hash outputs. Incorrect hashes would set bloom bits at wrong positions, causing false negatives in log search.

reachability.notes: All log emissions trigger bloom filter computation via keccak256. Incorrect hashes produce divergent blooms.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect keccak256 in bloom computation causes bloom filter divergence across clients, resulting in consensus split.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a keccak256 output that differs from the reference implementation for a specific input.

property.text: encode_receipt's type-dispatch logic MUST NOT silently encode receipts for unrecognized transaction types as legacy receipts. The isinstance fall-through path MUST only be reached for genuine LegacyTransaction instances.

reachability.notes: Silent fall-through for future transaction types would produce incorrect receipt encodings.

property.severity_justification: Silent misencoding of receipts for new transaction types causes consensus failures that are extremely difficult to debug.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: introduce a mock transaction type not in the isinstance chain and show encode_receipt produces legacy encoding.

property.text: A malicious block proposer MUST NOT be able to set a future timestamp for MEV advantages. The header timestamp MUST be strictly greater than the parent block's timestamp. forall (header, parent): header.timestamp > parent.timestamp.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer could manipulate the timestamp field within the block header.

property.severity_justification: Timestamp manipulation enables MEV extraction and disrupts block ordering invariants.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block with timestamp <= parent.timestamp that passes header validation.

property.text: A proposer MUST NOT be able to construct a block that inflates bloom filter and receipt processing overhead beyond gas-bounded limits. Log emission is bounded by the block gas limit and LOG opcode gas costs.

reachability.notes: An attacker could submit many log-heavy transactions to maximize bloom filter processing time.

property.severity_justification: Excessive log processing slows block validation but is bounded by gas limits.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block that causes disproportionate bloom filter processing time relative to gas consumed.

property.text: Block contents MUST be non-repudiable: the block header's parent_hash, state_root, transactions_root, receipt_root, and withdrawals_root commit to the full block contents via Merkle Patricia Trie roots. The consensus layer signs blocks via BLS signatures, binding proposer identity to the block.

reachability.notes: All block contents are committed via cryptographic roots in the header.

property.severity_justification: Repudiation resistance is important for accountability but does not directly enable attacks.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block whose committed roots do not match the actual block contents.

property.text: Corrupted withdrawal instructions from the consensus layer MUST NOT credit incorrect amounts to wrong addresses. The withdrawals_root in the header MUST commit to the exact withdrawal list.

reachability.notes: The Engine API is authenticated via JWT, but a compromised CL could inject invalid withdrawals.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect withdrawal processing causes unauthorized ETH minting, resulting in fund loss across the network.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that corrupted withdrawal data passes validation and credits incorrect amounts.

property.text: An attacker at the consensus-execution interface MUST NOT be able to inject fabricated withdrawal instructions not authorized by the beacon chain. The Engine API MUST use JWT authentication and the parent_beacon_block_root MUST provide a cryptographic anchor.

reachability.notes: Engine API is authenticated but an attacker with JWT access could spoof withdrawals.

property.severity_justification: Unauthorized ETH minting via fabricated withdrawals is a critical fund loss vulnerability.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate injection of unauthorized withdrawals via the Engine API.

property.text: A contract MUST NOT be able to emit excessive logs that cause bloom filter construction to process a number of add_to_bloom calls that exceeds what the block gas limit permits. forall (block): total_add_to_bloom_calls(block) <= gas_bounded_maximum.

reachability.notes: Each LOG opcode charges gas proportional to topics and data size, naturally limiting total logs per block.

property.severity_justification: Excessive log processing is bounded by gas limits but could still cause disproportionate processing time.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block where bloom filter processing time is disproportionate to gas consumed.

property.text: A transaction's fields (gas, value, data, to, nonce) MUST NOT be modifiable in transit between the sender and the execution engine. The ECDSA signature covers the full transaction payload and any modification invalidates the signature.

reachability.notes: Transaction integrity is enforced by ECDSA signatures. Any modification invalidates the signature.

property.severity_justification: Transaction field modification would allow unauthorized fund transfers or contract interactions.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a modified transaction that passes ECDSA signature verification.

property.text: SetCodeTransaction (EIP-7702) authorizations MUST be individually signed by the authorizing account. The execution engine MUST validate each authorization signature before granting code delegation privileges.

reachability.notes: EIP-7702 allows code delegation for EOAs. Weak authorization validation enables account takeover.

property.severity_justification: Unauthorized code delegation allows an attacker to execute arbitrary code under the victim's account identity, enabling full fund theft.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate an EIP-7702 authorization with a forged signature that is accepted by the execution engine.

property.text: RLP encoding of accounts via encode_account MUST maintain the canonical tuple ordering (nonce, balance, storage_root, code_hash). Any deviation from this ordering causes consensus incompatibility.

reachability.notes: Account encoding affects the state root. Non-canonical encoding produces a divergent state root.

property.severity_justification: Non-canonical account RLP encoding produces a different state_root, causing a network-wide consensus split.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate an account encoding with incorrect field ordering that produces a valid-looking but divergent state root.

property.text: The deposit contract address check in parse_deposit_requests MUST use exact equality: log.address == DEPOSIT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS. No other contract address MUST be accepted for deposit event parsing.

reachability.notes: A malicious contract could emit logs mimicking the deposit event signature at a different address.

property.severity_justification: Accepting deposit events from non-canonical addresses allows injection of fraudulent deposits into the consensus layer.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: deploy a contract that emits deposit-like events and demonstrate they are accepted by parse_deposit_requests.

property.text: extract_deposit_data MUST validate the total data length to be exactly 576 bytes and all five field offsets (PUBKEY_OFFSET=160, WC_OFFSET=256, AMOUNT_OFFSET=320, SIGNATURE_OFFSET=384, INDEX_OFFSET=512) and sizes (48, 32, 8, 96, 8) against expected constants. Any deviation MUST raise InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: A compromised deposit contract could emit malformed data. The extraction function must validate rigorously.

property.severity_justification: Accepting malformed deposit data corrupts the deposit request stream and could crash the consensus layer.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: emit a deposit event with data length != 576 or incorrect offsets and show it passes extract_deposit_data.

property.text: compute_requests_hash MUST produce identical output for identical input across all execution client implementations. The SHA-256 two-level construction (hash each request individually, then feed digests into outer hash) MUST be deterministic.

reachability.notes: Different hash outputs across clients cause block rejection at the CL, preventing finalization.

property.severity_justification: Non-deterministic requests_hash causes CL-EL disagreement and prevents block finalization across the network.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that two client implementations produce different requests_hash for identical input.

property.text: EIP-7702 Authorization MUST include chain_id for cross-chain replay protection and nonce for same-chain replay protection. An authorization replayed on a different chain or with a stale nonce MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: An attacker can capture a valid authorization and replay it on another chain or after a nonce increment.

property.severity_justification: Authorization replay enables unauthorized code delegation, leading to full account compromise and fund theft.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: capture a valid EIP-7702 authorization and replay it on a different chain or after nonce changes.

property.text: The storage_root parameter passed to encode_account MUST be the correct 32-byte Merkle root of the account's storage trie. encode_account DOES NOT validate this value; the caller MUST provide the correct root.

reachability.notes: The caller must ensure storage_root correctness. An internal bug could provide an incorrect root.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect storage_root corrupts the state trie and causes consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires an internal caller to provide an incorrect storage_root. Not directly externally reachable.

property.text: Type aliases (Address=Bytes20, Root=Hash32, VersionedHash=Hash32, Bloom=Bytes256) MUST enforce byte-length constraints at construction time. forall (addr : Address): len(addr) == 20; forall (root : Root): len(root) == 32; forall (bloom : Bloom): len(bloom) == 256.

reachability.notes: If type alias length enforcement is weak, malformed data could propagate through the system.

property.severity_justification: Weak type enforcement allows malformed data to propagate, causing downstream errors or consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct an Address with len != 20 and demonstrate it is accepted by the type system.

property.text: Receipt processing order (block_output.receipt_keys) MUST deterministically define the deposit request ordering. All compliant execution clients MUST iterate receipt keys in the same order to produce identical deposit_requests bytes.

reachability.notes: A malicious block builder controls receipt_keys ordering. Non-deterministic ordering causes requests_hash divergence.

property.severity_justification: Non-deterministic deposit ordering causes requests_hash mismatch, preventing block finalization.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate two clients producing different deposit request orderings for the same block.

property.text: trie_get MUST always return a valid (non-None) receipt for every key in block_output.receipt_keys. A None return MUST trigger an assertion failure, halting execution rather than silently skipping deposits.

reachability.notes: A crafted block with invalid receipt keys could trigger the assertion, crashing the client.

property.severity_justification: A crafted block that triggers the assertion failure crashes the execution client, causing a DoS.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a block where a receipt key has no corresponding entry in the receipts trie and demonstrate client crash.

property.text: A transaction sender MUST NOT be able to impersonate a different sender. The recover_sender function MUST use SECP256K1 signature recovery to derive the sender address from the transaction signature. Only the holder of the private key can produce a valid signature.

reachability.notes: Transaction sender authentication is fundamental to Ethereum security.

property.severity_justification: Sender impersonation enables unauthorized fund transfers and contract interactions.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a transaction with a forged signature that recovers to a different sender address.

property.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, the ECDSA signature (y_parity, r, s) MUST be verified against the claimed authorizing account before any code delegation is applied. If ecrecover(signature, message) != authorizer_address, the authorization MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft EIP-7702 set-code transactions with forged Authorization signatures and submit them via P2P or transaction pool. The Authorization tuple (chain_id, address, nonce, y_parity, r, s) is entirely attacker-controlled.

property.severity_justification: Bypass of ECDSA signature verification would allow an attacker to impersonate any account and set arbitrary code on it, enabling complete theft of funds and account takeover across the entire network.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating authorization forgery leading to unauthorized code delegation on a victim account.

property.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, the s-value of the ECDSA signature MUST be in the lower half of the secp256k1 curve order (s <= secp256k1n/2). Signatures with s > secp256k1n/2 MUST be rejected to prevent signature malleability.

reachability.notes: Attacker controls the (y_parity, r, s) values in the Authorization tuple. A malleable signature could pass verification but recover to a different address.

property.severity_justification: Signature malleability could allow authorization replay or recovery to an unintended address, leading to unauthorized code delegation.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating signature malleability leading to unintended authorization recovery.

property.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, ecrecover MUST NOT return address(0). If the recovered address is the zero address, the authorization MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft invalid signatures that cause ecrecover to return address(0), potentially granting code execution authority over the zero address.

property.severity_justification: If ecrecover returns address(0) and this is not rejected, attacker could set code on the zero address or bypass authorization checks entirely.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating ecrecover returning address(0) and authorization succeeding.

property.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, the chain_id field MUST match the current chain's ID. If chain_id != current_chain_id AND chain_id != 0, the authorization MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker can replay an Authorization signed for one chain on a different chain if chain_id validation is missing. The chain_id field is directly attacker-controlled.

property.severity_justification: Cross-chain replay of EIP-7702 authorizations would allow an attacker to delegate code on a victim's account on any chain where the authorization is accepted, enabling fund theft across chains.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating cross-chain replay of an Authorization tuple signed for a different chain_id.

property.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, the nonce field MUST equal the current nonce of the authorizing account at the time of processing. If authorization.nonce != account.nonce, the authorization MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker can attempt to replay a previously-used Authorization by resubmitting a transaction containing an old Authorization with a stale nonce.

property.severity_justification: Nonce bypass would allow replay of expired authorizations, potentially re-delegating code on accounts that have since revoked delegation.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating authorization replay after the authorizer's nonce has incremented.

property.text: After successful EIP-7702 authorization processing, the authorizer's account nonce MUST be incremented. forall (auth in processed_authorizations): post(account[auth.authorizer].nonce) == pre(account[auth.authorizer].nonce) + 1.

reachability.notes: If the nonce is not incremented after authorization, the same authorization can be replayed in subsequent transactions.

property.severity_justification: Failure to increment nonce allows unlimited replay of a single authorization, enabling persistent unauthorized code delegation.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing the same authorization accepted in two separate transactions.

property.text: When multiple EIP-7702 Authorizations within a single transaction target the same authorizer address, the resulting state MUST be deterministic across all EL implementations. The last valid authorization for a given authorizer MUST take precedence.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft a transaction with multiple conflicting Authorizations for the same account. If implementations handle conflicts differently, consensus divergence results.

property.severity_justification: Non-deterministic handling of duplicate authorizations could cause consensus divergence between EL implementations, leading to a network split.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating different state outcomes across two EL implementations when processing duplicate authorizations.

property.text: The deposit event filter in parse_deposit_requests MUST check BOTH log.address == DEPOSIT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS AND log.topics[0] == DEPOSIT_EVENT_SIGNATURE_HASH. Logs failing either check MUST be excluded from deposit request accumulation.

reachability.notes: Attacker can deploy a contract at a different address that emits events with the same topic hash. If the address check is missing, spoofed deposits would be included.

property.severity_justification: Missing address check would allow spoofed deposit events from arbitrary contracts, injecting fake deposits into the CL and potentially minting validator balances.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating a spoofed deposit event from a non-deposit contract being accepted into the deposit requests.

property.text: The parse_deposit_requests function MUST only process logs where len(log.topics) > 0 AND log.topics[0] == DEPOSIT_EVENT_SIGNATURE_HASH. Logs with empty topics or non-matching first topic MUST be skipped.

reachability.notes: A crafted transaction can cause the deposit contract to emit logs with unexpected topics. If topic filtering is missing, non-deposit events could be processed as deposits.

property.severity_justification: Processing non-deposit events as deposit data would produce incorrect requests hash, causing consensus divergence between EL implementations.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating a non-deposit log event being parsed as a deposit request.

property.text: The extract_deposit_data function MUST reject data where len(data) != DEPOSIT_EVENT_LENGTH (576 bytes) by raising InvalidBlock. forall (log_data): len(log_data) != 576 => extract_deposit_data(log_data) raises InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft transactions that cause the deposit contract to emit events with non-standard data lengths. The length check is the first line of defense.

property.severity_justification: Accepting deposit data of incorrect length could cause buffer overflows in implementations, consensus divergence, or parsing of garbage data as deposit requests.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating acceptance of deposit data with incorrect length.

property.text: The extract_deposit_data function MUST validate that the pubkey offset equals PUBKEY_OFFSET constant. If Uint.from_be_bytes(data[0:32]) != PUBKEY_OFFSET, the function MUST raise InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: Attacker controls the ABI-encoded deposit data offsets. A manipulated offset could cause extraction from wrong positions.

property.severity_justification: Bypassing offset validation could allow extraction of data from unintended positions, producing malformed deposit requests and consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating deposit data accepted with invalid pubkey offset.
